title: Diplocaulobium centrale
taxonomy: Diplocaulobium centrale is a species of orchid native to New Guinea. This orchid species belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Diplocaulobium is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae, which comprises many species of orchids. Within the Orchidaceae family, Diplocaulobium centrale is further classified as a member of the tribe Dendrobieae. This classification reflects the orchid's growth habit and other characteristics that distinguish it from other species within the family.

intro: Diplocaulobium centrale, also known as Centrale Dendrobium, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of the Pacific island of New Caledonia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
